rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 13, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Intervenor Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's Motion for Summary Judgment is off calendar per the request of moving party. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: STANLEY BOREN VS. GENERAL ELECTRIC COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 13, 2018 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant P.E. O'Hair & Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendants successfully invoked the devoid prong of Agular v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. "A threshold issue in asbestos litigation is exposure to the defendant's product." (McGonnell v. Kaiser Gypsum Co, Inc. (2002) 98 Cal.App.4th 1098, 1103.) "If there has been no exposure there is no causation." (Id. [citing Dumin v. Owens-Corning Fiberglas Corp. (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 650, 655.) Defendant demonstrates that the Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that he was exposed asbestos from gaskets supplied by Defendant. Plaintiff admits that he never personally worked with the gaskets nor observed others working with the gaskets in his vicinity. (Depo. Boren at pp. 170:20-25, 550:11-21, 553:3-9, 951:17-952:11, 952:20-952:14, 958:3-6.) Plaintiff could only determine that boxes of gaskets delivered by Defendant arrived full and then later became empty. (Depo. Boren at pp. 554:3-8, 1958:7-25, 2003:16-24.) Plaintiffs cannot prove that exposure to the gaskets supplied by Defendant constituted a substantial factor in causing Plaintiff Stanley Boren's asbestos related injury. Defendant shifted the burden, yet Plaintiffs offered no evidence nor addressed whether there are any triable issues of material fact. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
